title: Text, Unicode, Formatting, and Parsing · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: Normalize multilingual community-event records and generate readable confirmations. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a multilingual event text normalizer and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create string literals with correct quoting, escapes, raw forms, multiline content, and Unicode characters.
Use string indexing, slicing, membership, length, and immutability without off-by-one assumptions.
Choose search, split, join, strip, replace, case, and predicate methods from the actual text contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Python Regex Boundaries evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Python Regex Boundaries assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use regular expressions for justified pattern structure while preferring simpler string operations for simple parsing. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

A defensible solution applies Python Regex Boundaries, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a multilingual event text normalizer while every earlier competency remains active.
